meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: API Integration Guide to Ship Faster
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client projects. Master API integration with this beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster than ever.
content.answer_block: To overcome project paralysis, treat API integrations as modular, isolated services. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, you reduce cognitive load, enforce clean separation of concerns, and drastically accelerate your delivery cycle, allowing you to ship production-ready features in hours instead of days.
content.intro:
You’re staring at the cursor again. The client project is looming, the deadline is a phantom weight on your shoulders, and the API documentation looks like a foreign language. You’ve built the UI, but the backend integration feels like a brick wall you simply can't break through. This is the 'freelancer’s stall'—where great ideas go to die in unfinished GitHub repositories.

But what if the integration wasn't a manual slog? By shifting your workflow from 'writing code' to 'orchestrating agents,' you turn the most intimidating part of the build into your competitive advantage. It’s time to stop fighting the syntax and start shipping the solution.

steps.title: Define the API Schema
steps.description: Before touching code, map the input and output requirements. This prevents feature creep and ensures your agent knows exactly what to fetch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, typed interface definition for a REST API client that handles authentication headers and error handling for the [API Name] endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./docs/api-spec.md and generate a types.ts file for the integration.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Integration Layer
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate, ensuring your project follows modern best practices for async/await patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular API service class in TypeScript that implements axios, includes a retry mechanism for 5xx errors, and uses environment variables for keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npx tsc --init' then create src/services/api.ts

steps.title: Implement Authentication Logic
steps.description: Security is where most beginners fail. We abstract auth tokens into a middleware layer so your main logic stays clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure interceptor for the API service that injects Bearer tokens and handles token refresh logic automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/services/api.ts to include the auth interceptor logic.

steps.title: Automated Testing of Endpoints
steps.description: Don't test manually in the browser. Use Claude Code to generate mock tests that validate your API responses against the schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of Vitest unit tests for the API service that mock the network layer and assert correct data transformation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'generate unit tests for api.ts'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Finalize the integration by setting up basic logging to track API health, ensuring you catch issues before your client does.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a simple logging utility to the API service that captures request latency and error codes for debugging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm run build' and verify the integration output.

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated 'adapter' pattern to decouple the API response from your frontend state to make future migrations painless.
Never hardcode API keys; use a .env file and validate them at runtime to fail fast if configuration is missing.
Use Claude Code to generate JSDoc comments for every API method—it makes your code self-documenting for the client.
Implement request caching via local storage or a light state manager to minimize API calls and stay within rate limits.
Treat every API integration as a 'black box'—if the API changes, you only have to update one service file, not your entire app.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is live, move to implementing a global error-handling boundary to ensure your UI gracefully handles failed requests.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting incomplete projects define your freelance career. With the right tooling and this modular approach, you can outpace your competition and deliver high-value work consistently. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
